safety_plan_metadata.description: Structured safety plan based on the Stanley-Brown Safety Planning Intervention. Designed for collaborative completion between patient and provider during a therapy session. All fields should be reviewed and updated periodically.

step_1_warning_signs.instructions: List the thoughts, feelings, moods, images, situations, or behaviors that indicate a crisis may be developing. These are the personal cues that tell you it's time to use this safety plan.

warning_signs.description: Feeling like a burden to my family and friends
Withdrawing from text messages and social plans for more than 2 days
Difficulty getting out of bed combined with not eating
Intrusive images of giving up or disappearing
Sudden sense of calm after a long period of intense distress (potential resolve to act)
Increased alcohol use — more than 2 drinks in a sitting

step_2_coping_strategies.instructions: List activities you can do on your own to take your mind off problems or help you feel better, without contacting another person. These should be things you can do independently.

strategies.activity: Go for a walk in the neighborhood, at least 15 minutes
strategies.notes: Works best in daylight; bring headphones with calming playlist

strategies.activity: Box breathing exercise — 4 counts in, 4 hold, 4 out, 4 hold — repeat 5 cycles
strategies.notes: Can do anywhere; sometimes need to do 8-10 cycles

strategies.activity: Write in journal using the 3-column thought record
strategies.notes: Journal is in nightstand drawer; use the CBT template Dr. Kim provided
Most effective coping strategy; keeps hands and mind occupied

strategies.activity: Cold water on wrists and face (grounding technique)
strategies.notes: Helpful for acute distress; follow with breathing exercise

strategies.activity: Watch comfort show (Great British Bake Off) — max 2 episodes
strategies.notes: Set a timer; avoid doom-scrolling on phone afterward

step_3_social_contacts_for_distraction.instructions: List people and social settings that can help provide distraction from a crisis. You do not need to disclose that you are in crisis. The goal is to be around others.

contacts.name: Lucia Torres (sister)
contacts.notes: Usually available evenings and weekends; can suggest getting coffee or going on a walk

contacts.name: Dev Khatri (close friend)
contacts.notes: Lives nearby; good for spontaneous hangouts; knows about treatment generally

contacts.name: Community yoga class at Bend Studio
contacts.notes: No need to talk; just being around people helps; $15 drop-in

step_4_people_to_ask_for_help.instructions: List people you can tell that you are in crisis and ask for help. These are trusted individuals who know about your mental health and can provide support.

trusted_contacts.name: Lucia Torres (sister)
trusted_contacts.notes: Has a copy of this safety plan; knows to help remove access to alcohol and drive to ER if needed

trusted_contacts.name: Roberto Torres (father)
trusted_contacts.notes: Aware of diagnosis and treatment; can come to apartment within 30 minutes

trusted_contacts.name: Dr. Sarah Kim (therapist)
trusted_contacts.notes: Available during business hours M-F 9am-5pm; has voicemail for after-hours with callback within 2 hours on weekdays

trusted_contacts.name: Dr. Anish Patel (psychiatrist)
trusted_contacts.notes: For medication concerns or if symptoms are escalating rapidly; office has after-hours on-call coverage

step_5_professional_and_crisis_resources.instructions: These are professional resources available 24/7 for crisis support. You can contact them at any time.

crisis_resources.name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
crisis_resources.description: Free, confidential support for people in distress. Call or text 988.

crisis_resources.name: Crisis Text Line
crisis_resources.description: Free text-based crisis support with trained counselors.

crisis_resources.name: Nearest Emergency Department
crisis_resources.notes: 15-minute drive or 25-minute subway from apartment; sister Lucia can drive

step_6_environmental_safety.instructions: Steps to make the environment safer by reducing access to lethal means. These steps should be taken proactively, not just during a crisis.

safety_steps.action: Remove alcohol from apartment and ask Lucia to hold it
safety_steps.notes: Lucia stores wine/spirits at her place; Maya will tell Lucia before purchasing any alcohol

safety_steps.action: Store medications in a timed lock box (Kitchen Safe)
safety_steps.notes: Kitchen Safe set to dispense one day's dose at a time; extra medication stored at Dad's house

safety_steps.action: Remove or secure sharp objects in bathroom
safety_steps.notes: Switched to electric razor; box cutter moved to Lucia's apartment

safety_steps.action: Keep phone charged and nearby at night
safety_steps.notes: Charging station on nightstand; safety plan saved as phone lock screen favorites

reasons_for_living.instructions: List the things that are most important to you and that give you reasons to keep going. Review these when warning signs appear.
reasons: My sister Lucia — she depends on me and I depend on her; we promised to always be there for each other
My goal of finishing my MFA in creative writing — I have one semester left and I want to see my thesis through
My guitar and songwriting — I haven't shared my songs yet and I want to perform at an open mic
My cat Pepper — she needs me and greets me every morning
The progress I've already made in therapy — I don't want to lose what I've built
Wanting to travel to Oaxaca with my dad like we've planned since I was a kid

provider_notes.clinical_observations: Maya collaboratively developed this plan during session on 2026-01-10. She was engaged and identified coping strategies with specificity. Primary lethal means concern is medication overdose and alcohol — both have been addressed with means restriction. Sister Lucia is a strong protective factor. Plan should be reviewed at each session and updated as needed. Maya has a laminated copy in her wallet and digital copy on her phone.

plan_modifications_log.modification: Added Dev Khatri as social contact; updated coping strategy effectiveness ratings based on past month's experience; confirmed all means restriction steps still in place
